title: Supported Cloud Providers in CloudDash
content:
CloudDash aggregates metrics, logs, and alerts from major cloud providers into a unified monitoring dashboard. Understanding supported providers and their integration depth helps you plan your observability stack.

AWS: Full support for CloudWatch Metrics, CloudWatch Logs, CloudWatch Alarms, and AWS X-Ray traces. CloudDash uses IAM role assumption via cross-account trust policies. Supported services include EC2, ECS, EKS, Lambda, RDS, S3, ELB, and API Gateway. Metric polling interval is configurable from 1 to 5 minutes depending on your plan tier.

Google Cloud Platform: Integration with Cloud Monitoring (formerly Stackdriver), Cloud Logging, and Cloud Trace. CloudDash connects via a service account with Monitoring Viewer and Logging Viewer roles. GKE cluster auto-discovery is available on Pro and Enterprise plans.

Microsoft Azure: Support for Azure Monitor metrics, Log Analytics workspaces, and Application Insights. Authentication uses Azure AD app registrations with Reader role on the subscription scope. Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) monitoring is fully supported.

Additional integrations include Kubernetes (any distribution via Prometheus remote write), Datadog (metric import), PagerDuty (alert routing), and Slack/Microsoft Teams (notification channels). CloudDash does not currently support on-premises VMware or bare-metal SNMP polling natively, though you can push custom metrics via our Metrics Ingest API.

Starter plan supports one cloud provider connection. Pro supports up to three. Enterprise supports unlimited connections with dedicated integration engineering for custom data sources. When adding a provider, CloudDash runs a connectivity test that validates credentials and permissions before saving the integration.
